warnings: ABAP COMMIT WORK behavior differs from Snowflake auto-commit - transaction control may behave differently
ABAP sy-datum system variable converted to CURRENT_DATE - timezone handling may differ
ABAP VALUE # constructor syntax converted to standard INSERT VALUES syntax
Error handling and rollback behavior may not match SAP runtime exactly
Hardcoded sales_order value '5000012345' should be parameterized for production use
assumptions: Tables zsales_order and zsales_order_log exist in Snowflake with compatible column structures
Column data types are compatible between SAP and Snowflake environments
No client (MANDT) handling required based on typed_ast analysis
CURRENT_DATE provides acceptable substitute for SAP sy-datum system field
Snowflake procedure exception handling provides adequate error management
conversion_notes: Converted ABAP REPORT to Snowflake stored procedure to preserve transactional UPDATE/INSERT sequence
Replaced SAP sy-datum system variable with Snowflake CURRENT_DATE function
Added explicit exception handling with rollback capability
Converted ABAP VALUE # inline constructor to standard SQL INSERT VALUES syntax
Maintained original business logic: update order status then log the action
Procedure can be called via CALL z_update_order_status() or scheduled as a task

applied_rules.before:
REPORT z_update_order_status.

UPDATE zsales_order
SET status = 'COMPLETED',
    changed_on = sy-datum
WHERE sales_order = '5000012345'.

COMMIT WORK.



INSERT zsales_order_log FROM VALUE #(
    sales_order = '5000012345'
    action      = 'UPDATED'
    created_on  = sy-datum ).
applied_rules.after:
REPORT z_update_order_status.

UPDATE zsales_order
SET status = 'COMPLETED',
    changed_on = CURRENT_DATE
WHERE sales_order = '5000012345'.

COMMIT WORK.



INSERT zsales_order_log FROM VALUE #(
    sales_order = '5000012345'
    action      = 'UPDATED'
    created_on  = CURRENT_DATE ).
